visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a vibrant graphic intro featuring the Costco Wholesale logo and a 2D animated character with an exaggerated shocked expression. Large yellow and blue text overlays dominate the center, set against a blurred warehouse background with high-shelf racks and industrial lighting. (2) The scene cuts rapidly to a stack of Kleenex tissue boxes on a warehouse pallet. The lighting is bright and even, highlighting the purple packaging and the white text overlay showing pricing details. (3) A cut follows to refrigerated packs of raw chicken thighs. The pinkish-red meat is visible through clear plastic vacuum packaging, arranged in neat rows under bright retail lighting. (4) The sequence continues with various packaged goods: Viva brand snack boxes stacked high, I-Mei egg roll tins in bright red, and Haribo candy tubs in green and blue containers. Each shot is a static, medium-full shot of the products on their respective warehouse shelves. (5) Subsequent shots feature a variety of food and household items, including bags of Lotte snacks, boxes of Lindt chocolates, and stacks of Lenor laundry scent beads. The camera remains mostly static for each product, with rapid straight cuts between them. (6) The final series of shots covers frozen and chilled sections, showing seafood like squid in trays, pre-made meals in black plastic containers, and cases of bottled water and beer. The industrial warehouse setting remains consistent throughout, with bright, high-contrast lighting and a practical, commercial aesthetic.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: No real people are present in the video focus; the environment contains only products and warehouse shelves.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The video is filmed within a Costco warehouse. Backgrounds include industrial high-bay racking systems, walk-in refrigeration units, and pallet displays of bulk goods.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio starts with a high-energy male narrator speaking rapidly in Traditional Chinese. A prominent 'pop' sound effect marks the transition. The BGM is a high-tempo, major-mode electronic pop track with a strong 4/4 beat. (2) Every two seconds, a 'ding' or 'pop' sound effect triggers as the narrator announces a new product and its price. The vocal delivery is consistently high-pitched and enthusiastic, with very short pauses between words. (3) The background music maintains a steady rhythmic pulse throughout, while the narrator's voice sits clearly above the music mix. There are no ambient environmental noises, suggesting a studio-processed vocal track. (4) The sequence of product announcements continues at a rapid-fire pace, with the 'pop' sound effect recurring on every visual cut. The audio density is extremely high, with near-constant speech-music overlap and a driving, promotional energy. (5) As the video nears its end, the BGM remains consistent in tempo and volume, while the narrator continues to enunciate product names and prices with high clarity and arousal until the final track cut.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a rapid-fire promotional guide for weekly discounts at Costco Wholesale in Taiwan. It aims to inform viewers about specific price drops and encourage them to visit the store or follow the creator's social media for future updates.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video features a massive list of products with specific price details and the Costco logo, formatted as a consumer guide.

high_inference.freeDescribe: This is a typical social media 'deal-finder' style video, characterized by its hyper-fast editing and high information density intended to hold viewer attention on mobile platforms.
